channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: Massive boom over northeastern US was a meteor explosion as powerful as 300 tons of TNT, NASA confirms - Space
item.description:
1. Massive boom over northeastern US was a meteor explosion as powerful as 300 tons of TNT, NASA confirms  Space
  2. 5-foot meteor entered Earth’s atmosphere, sending meteorite falling into Cape Cod Bay, NASA says  WHDH
  3. Can meteorites be recovered from Cape Cod Bay? Harvard professor says "it's just like a fishing expedition"  CBS News
  4. Meteor Explosion Caught on Camera in Rome  WKTV
  5. Meteor as heavy as an elephant causes widespread speculation across New England  AP News

item.title: Webb Telescope snaps interstellar comet – proves it's like nothing we've ever seen in our own Solar System - BBC Sky at Night Magazine
item.description:
1. Webb Telescope snaps interstellar comet – proves it's like nothing we've ever seen in our own Solar System  BBC Sky at Night Magazine
  2. NASA’s Webb Detects Methane on Interstellar Comet 3I/ATLAS  NASA Science (.gov)
  3. Webb sniffs methane from interstellar Comet 3I/ATLAS  European Space Agency
  4. Webb Detects Methane on Interstellar Comet 3I/ATLAS  Sci.News
  5. Interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS just turned out to carry 40 times more heavy water than Earth’s oceans — chemistry impossible to make inside our solar system  MSN

item.title: NASA Crowns New Leader in Race to Colonize the Moon. It’s Not SpaceX - Yahoo Finance
item.description:
1. NASA Crowns New Leader in Race to Colonize the Moon. It’s Not SpaceX  Yahoo Finance

  4. NASA Goes Big: 3 Moon Missions In 2026 To Start Lunar Base  The Weather Channel
  5. NASA selects four companies for initial moon base awards  SpaceNews

item.title: Astronomers discover exoplanets with magnetic fields - Reuters
item.description:
1. Astronomers discover exoplanets with magnetic fields  Reuters
  2. Strange winds reveal strongest hints yet of magnetic activity in exoplanets  Eso.org
  3. Magnetic shields on distant worlds offer clue in hunt for alien life  thetimes.com
  4. Scientists left stunned by strange phenomenon on distant planets  The Independent
  5. Magnetic field strengths of hot giant exoplanets consistent with Solar System values  Nature

item.title: May's Blue Moon wows stargazers worldwide — see these stunning photos of the smallest full moon of 2026 - Space
item.description:

item.title: Periodic radio and X-ray emission from an accreting white dwarf binary - Nature
item.description:
1. Periodic radio and X-ray emission from an accreting white dwarf binary  Nature

  3. Astronomers might have decoded a mysterious signal from space  The Independent

  5. First Proof Mysterious Radio Pulses Come From Cataclysmic Variables Represents “Astronomical Rosetta Stone”  IFLScience

item.title: Mars was once a warmer world of rivers, lakes and a thicker atmosphere, but after its internal dynamo died and the planet lost the magnetic shield that helps protect an atmosphere, the solar wind stripped much of its air away over billions of years, leaving the c - Space Daily
item.description:
1. Mars was once a warmer world of rivers, lakes and a thicker atmosphere, but after its internal dynamo died and the planet lost the magnetic shield that helps protect an atmosphere, the solar wind stripped much of its air away over billions of years, leaving the c  Space Daily
  2. NASA Uses Mineralogical Marker to Understand Ancient Martian Climate  NASA Science (.gov)
  3. Mars Hid its Warm, Wet Crystals Underground  Universe Today
  4. Carbon-Rich Rocks May Have Cooled the Ancient Martian Atmosphere  eos.org
  5. Mars’s Gale Crater Would Have Been Habitable For Millions Of Years  Orbital Today

item.title: This fish species survived 100,000 years without males. Scientists thought it should be long dead – but it's thriving - BBC

item.title: One of our planets may be missing, and it could explain why the solar system looks the way it does - Phys.org
item.description:
1. One of our planets may be missing, and it could explain why the solar system looks the way it does  Phys.org
  2. An unknown planet in the Solar System destroyed Uranus's moons  Universe Space Tech
  3. A 'lost planet' may have given Jupiter and Uranus their moons  Space
  4. The mystery of a planet that disappeared billions of years ago has been revealed  AzEdu.az

item.title: Matter may entangle with light far more easily near quantum critical points - Phys.org
item.description: Matter may entangle with light far more easily near quantum critical points  Phys.org

item.title: About 63 light-years away there is a deep-blue world that looks deceptively like Earth from a distance, but on the planet HD 189733b the temperature reaches 2,000 degrees Fahrenheit and the winds scream at thousands of miles an hour - Space Daily
item.description:
1. About 63 light-years away there is a deep-blue world that looks deceptively like Earth from a distance, but on the planet HD 189733b the temperature reaches 2,000 degrees Fahrenheit and the winds scream at thousands of miles an hour  Space Daily
  2. There is a planet 63 light-years from Earth where the rain is made of molten glass, the winds blow at 7,000 kilometres per hour, the daytime temperature is over 1,000 degrees Celsius, and the planet itself, viewed from space, is the same deep blue as Earth.  Space Daily

item.description:
1. CERN Finds High-Significance Hint of Physics Beyond Standard Model  Ground News

  3. Large Hadron Collider detects strange particle behavior that could rewrite physics  ScienceDaily

  5. Analysis of B-meson decay hints at new physics  Physics Today

item.title: Parts of Canada are quietly short on gravity. The standard story blames an ice sheet that pressed the crust down and vanished thousands of years ago, but satellites suggest that explains less than half of it. The rest comes from something churning far deeper in - Space Daily
item.description: Parts of Canada are quietly short on gravity. The standard story blames an ice sheet that pressed the crust down and vanished thousands of years ago, but satellites suggest that explains less than half of it. The rest comes from something churning far deeper in  Space Daily

item.title: Astronomers discover a 'lost world' of black hole mergers: 'It's the astronomical equivalent of uncovering an ancient civilization' - Space
item.description:
1. Astronomers discover a 'lost world' of black hole mergers: 'It's the astronomical equivalent of uncovering an ancient civilization'  Space
  2. Gravitational wave astronomy is now a fully mature science  Big Think
  3. Scientists Stunned: Two Black Holes Captured Together in a Historic First  Futura, le média qui explore le monde
  4. The Universe Is Full of ‘Impossible’ Black Holes. Scientists Now Know Why  WIRED
  5. The ‘age of gravitational astronomy’ is here  Scientific American

item.title: Can Our Model of the Cosmos Work Without Dark Energy? New Research Says It Can - Gizmodo
item.description:
1. Can Our Model of the Cosmos Work Without Dark Energy? New Research Says It Can  Gizmodo

  3. Taking Dark Energy Out of the Equation  UC Davis
  4. Is Dark Energy Unnecessary? Mathematicians Challenge Standard Cosmological Model of Universe  Sci.News
  5. Did We Invent Dark Energy for Nothing?  Universe Today

item.title: Scientists Think a Cosmic Particle Accelerator Shot a Mystery Particle Right at Earth - Yahoo
item.description:
1. Scientists Think a Cosmic Particle Accelerator Shot a Mystery Particle Right at Earth  Yahoo
  2. Supermassive black holes are pointing jets of plasma directly at Earth — and a population of them may have produced the highest-energy neutrino ever recorded  Space Daily
  3. Most powerful 'ghost particle' ever may have come from a cosmic particle accelerator fed by a black hole  Space
  4. Scientists may have found the source of the most powerful neutrino ever detected  ScienceDaily
  5. Most Powerful Neutrino Ever Detected May Have Come From a Blazar  gadgets360.com

item.title: Zombie Chunks of Sea Cucumber Refuse to Die, Raising Questions About What It Means to Live Forever - Gizmodo
item.description:
1. Zombie Chunks of Sea Cucumber Refuse to Die, Raising Questions About What It Means to Live Forever  Gizmodo
  2. A Sea Cucumber's Amputated Tissue Refuses To Die. Could It Live Forever?  ScienceAlert
  3. Amputated sea cucumber parts show signs of ‘immortality,’ researchers say  CNN
  4. Scientists Intrigued by Chunk of Flesh That Refuses to Die After Several Years  Yahoo
  5. Scientists discover 'zombie' sea cucumber that could outlive us all  Metro.co.uk

  2. The Genetic Secrets of a Shark That Lives for 500 Years  Nautilus | Science

item.title: When Rosetta sniffed the gas around Comet 67P, it found a cloud that would have smelled of rotten eggs, ammonia and bitter almonds — and hidden in that cosmic stink were some of the chemical ingredients that may have helped life begin on Earth - Space Daily
item.description: When Rosetta sniffed the gas around Comet 67P, it found a cloud that would have smelled of rotten eggs, ammonia and bitter almonds — and hidden in that cosmic stink were some of the chemical ingredients that may have helped life begin on Earth  Space Daily

item.title: Cats can't taste sweetness — evolution turned off the relevant gene in their distant ancestors when they became obligate carnivores, and without working sweet receptors, a cat is as indifferent to sugar as a person is to ultraviolet light - Space Daily
item.description: Cats can't taste sweetness — evolution turned off the relevant gene in their distant ancestors when they became obligate carnivores, and without working sweet receptors, a cat is as indifferent to sugar as a person is to ultraviolet light  Space Daily

item.title: In 2017, astronomers spotted the first object ever confirmed to have come from another star system passing through our own: a strange, elongated visitor called ‘Oumuamua that seemed to accelerate as it left, in a way scientists are still debating - Space Daily
item.description: In 2017, astronomers spotted the first object ever confirmed to have come from another star system passing through our own: a strange, elongated visitor called ‘Oumuamua that seemed to accelerate as it left, in a way scientists are still debating  Space Daily

item.title: There is a fish that can live more than a hundred years — the rougheye rockfish, which inhabits the deep waters of the North Pacific — and the slow rate at which it grows, breeds, and ages means that some of the individuals being caught by fishermen today w - Space Daily
item.description: There is a fish that can live more than a hundred years — the rougheye rockfish, which inhabits the deep waters of the North Pacific — and the slow rate at which it grows, breeds, and ages means that some of the individuals being caught by fishermen today w  Space Daily

item.title: Honeybees can recognize human faces — they can be trained to distinguish between individual humans by face and continue to recognize them across different viewpoints, despite having a brain smaller than the head of a pin - Space Daily
item.description: Honeybees can recognize human faces — they can be trained to distinguish between individual humans by face and continue to recognize them across different viewpoints, despite having a brain smaller than the head of a pin  Space Daily

item.title: There are about 20 quadrillion ants alive on Earth at any moment — enough that their combined biomass outweighs every wild bird and mammal on the planet combined, even though the often-repeated claim that ant biomass equals human biomass has been - Space Daily
item.description: There are about 20 quadrillion ants alive on Earth at any moment — enough that their combined biomass outweighs every wild bird and mammal on the planet combined, even though the often-repeated claim that ant biomass equals human biomass has been  Space Daily

item.title: The Sun is not standing still. It is carrying the entire Solar System around the centre of the Milky Way, and one lap takes roughly 230 million years. The last time we were this far around the galaxy, Earth was in the Triassic Period and the very first dinosaurs wer - Space Daily
item.description: The Sun is not standing still. It is carrying the entire Solar System around the centre of the Milky Way, and one lap takes roughly 230 million years. The last time we were this far around the galaxy, Earth was in the Triassic Period and the very first dinosaurs wer  Space Daily

item.title: Japanese Spacecraft Heading Towards Asteroid 1998 KY26 – But A New Paper Claims It Will Find An Object Of Technological Origin - IFLScience
item.description:
1. Japanese Spacecraft Heading Towards Asteroid 1998 KY26 – But A New Paper Claims It Will Find An Object Of Technological Origin  IFLScience
  2. Is the `Dark Comet’ 1998 KY26 the Spacecraft Phobos 1?  Avi Loeb – Medium
  3. Paper Claims the “Asteroid” Japan’s Probe Is Approaching Is Actually a Derelict Spacecraft  Futurism
  4. Scientist says THIS dark comet could be a 38-year-old human probe - What Avi Loeb told us  WION
  5. What’s up with 1998 KY26?  Initiative for Interstellar Studies
